title: Heartland vs. Rimland
gist: In the readable opening of this otherwise paywalled essay, Beckley and Brands frame today's geopolitics through a classic heartland-vs-rimland lens: a China-Russia-led bloc of continental autocracies (with Iran and North Korea) seeking to dominate Eurasia, against a US-anchored maritime order of coastal states.

insights.claim: China and Russia, reinforced by Iran and North Korea and ringed by autocracies from Belarus to Myanmar, now occupy the role of continental empires (like Napoleonic France, imperial Germany, and the USSR) seeking to dominate Eurasia and project power globally.
insights.speaker: Michael Beckley and Hal Brands
insights.so_what: Maps the current great-power contest onto a recurring continental-vs-maritime pattern, a frame for assessing alliance blocs and conflict risk.
insights.quote: continental empires seeking to dominate Eurasia and project power globally

insights.claim: The United States, like the United Kingdom before it, remains the only actor capable of anchoring a great arc of coastal and maritime countries against the Eurasian continental bloc.
insights.speaker: Michael Beckley and Hal Brands
insights.so_what: Casts the US as the indispensable maritime balancer; its retrenchment would leave the rimland coalition without an anchor.
insights.quote: remains the only actor capable of anchoring a great arc of coastal and maritime countries
insights.locator: Intro / 'The United States, like the United Kingdom before it'
